title: Electrical Power-Line Installers and Repairers
description: Install or repair cables or wires used in electrical power or distribution systems. May erect poles and light or heavy duty transmission towers.

tasks.text: Adhere to safety practices and procedures, such as checking equipment regularly and erecting barriers around work areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive vehicles equipped with tools and materials to job sites.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Open switches or attach grounding devices to remove electrical hazards from disturbed or fallen lines or to facilitate repairs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Climb poles or use truck-mounted buckets to access equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install, maintain, and repair electrical distribution and transmission systems, including conduits, cables, wires, and related equipment, such as transformers, circuit breakers, and switches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect and test power lines and auxiliary equipment to locate and identify problems, using reading and testing instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Coordinate work assignment preparation and completion with other workers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Replace or straighten damaged poles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: String wire conductors and cables between poles, towers, trenches, pylons, and buildings, setting lines in place and using winches to adjust tension.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach cross-arms, insulators, and auxiliary equipment to poles prior to installing them.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dig holes, using augers, and set poles, using cranes and power equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Travel in trucks, helicopters, and airplanes to inspect lines for freedom from obstruction and adequacy of insulation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Identify defective sectionalizing devices, circuit breakers, fuses, voltage regulators, transformers, switches, relays, or wiring, using wiring diagrams and electrical-testing instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install watt-hour meters and connect service drops between power lines and consumers' facilities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test conductors, according to electrical diagrams and specifications, to identify corresponding conductors and to prevent incorrect connections.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place insulating or fireproofing materials over conductors and joints.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Splice or solder cables together or to overhead transmission lines, customer service lines, or street light lines, using hand tools, epoxies, or specialized equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Trim trees that could be hazardous to the functioning of cables or wires.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pull up cable by hand from large reels mounted on trucks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay underground cable directly in trenches, or string it through conduit running through the trenches.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Cut trenches for laying underground cables, using trenchers and cable plows.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut and peel lead sheathing and insulation from defective or newly installed cables and conduits prior to splicing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Clean, tin, and splice corresponding conductors by twisting ends together or by joining ends with metal clamps and soldering connections.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
